title: dryopteris nitidula
intro: Dryopteris nitidula, also known as the smooth wood fern, is a species of fern native to eastern Asia, characterized by its lance-shaped fronds and smooth stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The smooth wood fern is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving headaches, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
